chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR C. P. No. D – 1572 of 2017 C. P. No. D – 1382 of 2017 C. P. No. D – 1573 of 2017 C. P. No. D – 1578 of 2017 C. P. No. D – 1580 of 2017 C. P. No. D – 1607 of 2017 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge Hearing of case (priority) 1. For hearing of CMA No.9816/2017 (S/A) 2. For hearing of main case 03-11-2021 Mr. Mukesh Kumar G. Karara, Advocate for the Petitioners in C. Ps. No. D-1572, 1573, 1578, 1580 and 1607 of 2017. Mr. Aamir Mustafa Kamario, Associate of Mr. Qurban Ali Malano, Advocate for the Petitioners in C. P. No. D-1382 of 2017. Mr. Zuber Ahmed Rajput, Advocate for Respondents-NHA. Mr. Zulfiqar Ali Naich, Assistant Advocate General Sindh. Mr. Muhammad Hamzo Buriro, Deputy Attorney General..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- The grievance of the petitioners is twofold; one in respect of quantum of amount being demanded by NHA in respect of certain approvals; second regarding requirement of NOC or otherwise in respect of construction of amenities on their respective properties. After making submissions, both learned Counsel have agreed for disposal of these Petitions by consent in the following terms: 1. NHA / concerned officials will decide the objections of the Petitioners with respect to Serial No.9, 10 and 11 under the head of Requirement as per Check List-II of notice dated 29-05-2017 as to the quantum of dues / issuance of NOC demanded by NHA in terms of the existing SOPs and regulations / rules, after providing opportunity of personal hearing to the Petitioners. Such exercise shall be carried out in respect of C. Ps. No. D-1572, 1573 and 1578 of 2017 preferably within a period of sixty days from today, and till such time the objections are decided, no coercive action be taken against the petitioners.

No. D-1572, 1573 and 1578 of 2017 preferably within a period of sixty days from today, and till such time the objections are decided, no coercive action be taken against the petitioners. If the petitioners are still aggrieved they may seek appropriate remedy in accordance with law. C. P. No. D – 1572 of 2017 and others 2 2. Insofar as C. Ps. No. D-1382, 1580 and 1607 of 2017 are concerned, again in the same manner, the objection regarding obtaining NOC for the purposes of any amenities constructed on the properties in question, NHA / concerned officials will decide the said objection in terms of the existing SOPs and regulations / rules, after providing opportunity of personal hearing to the Petitioners. Such exercise shall also be carried out preferably within a period of sixty days from today and till such time the objection is decided; no coercive action be taken against the petitioners. If the petitioners are still aggrieved they may seek appropriate remedy in accordance with law. With these observations, the Petitions are disposed of with pending application(s). Office is directed to place a signed copy of this order in the captioned connected matters. J U D G E J U D G E Abdul Basit
